steps.title: Rewrite to match the given root form
steps.content: Start with $$x^2+7x+5=0.$$ Multiply by 4 so the square will have integer coefficients: $$4x^2+28x+20=0.$$

steps.title: Complete the square
steps.content:
Move the constant and complete the square: $$4x^2+28x=-20$$ $$(2x+7)^2=29.$$ Therefore $$2x+7=\pm\sqrt{29},$$ so $$x=\frac{-7\pm\sqrt{29}}{2}.$$ In the given form, $k=29$.

The expression under the radical is the requested $k$, so once the completed-square form has $\sqrt{29}$, the value of $k$ is fixed.
